chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the source code in Section IX (Conclusion): https://zenodo.org/records/10784506.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on software simulations using QEMU 6.1.50 and Unikraft 0.8.0 running on a PowerEdge R740 server. There is no mention of physical hardware testbeds or massive-scale cloud deployments beyond the single machine used.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section II-B contains a dedicated, explicit section defining the Fault Model. It clearly distinguishes between fail-stop faults (recoverable via reboot/replay) and deterministic/hardware failures (not recoverable), providing precise assumptions about what the system can and cannot handle.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs in Section VII. It quantifies performance penalties (up to 1.46x overhead), memory space overhead (<200 MB), reboot times (<48 ms), and log entry counts for various system calls.

major_weakness: The experimental evaluation is limited to a single physical machine running QEMU simulations, lacking the large-scale deployment or diverse hardware environments typical of high-reliability system papers.
